description: Manufacturing Round 2 Run 5: Platform enhancements (Event Bus, Sub-table Summary, Excel Import, Bitemporal, Chain) + Integration verification

userStories.title: Implement Domain Event Bus using Spring ApplicationEvent
acceptanceCriteria: DomainEvent base class exists with tenantId, recordId, modelCode, payload, occurredAt
CommandCompletedEvent extends DomainEvent with commandCode and operationType
CommandEventLogger listens to events and logs them
userStories.notes: TDD required. Use @TransactionalEventListener(phase = AFTER_COMMIT) for listeners that should only fire after transaction commits (e.g., sending notifications). Use @EventListener for listeners that should fire within the same transaction (e.g., creating related records). The event publisher integration into the command pipeline is the most critical part — find the right extension point without modifying core command engine logic excessively.

userStories.title: Implement Sub-table Summary sideEffect (AGGREGATE type)
acceptanceCriteria: Supports SUM aggregation of child field to parent field
userStories.notes: TDD required. First investigate the existing sideEffect infrastructure in the command pipeline before implementing. The sideEffect might be triggered by a post-execution phase in the command engine. If no SideEffectHandler interface exists, create a minimal one. The AGGREGATE pattern is extremely common in ERP: SO header total = SUM(line amounts), WH In total = SUM(line amounts), etc.

userStories.title: Implement Excel Import framework
acceptanceCriteria: Supports: parse → validate → preview (dryRun) → insert workflow
Maps Excel column headers to field codes by displayName
Validates required fields and data types
userStories.notes: TDD required. Apache POI may or may not be in classpath — check build.gradle. If not present, add org.apache.poi:poi-ooxml dependency. For tests, create a small in-memory Excel file using POI's XSSFWorkbook. The column-to-field mapping should try: exact code match first, then zh-CN displayName match, then en displayName match.

userStories.title: Implement Bitemporal infrastructure (base class + repository)
acceptanceCriteria: BitemporalEntity base class exists with entityKey, validFrom/To, txnFrom/To fields
BitemporalRepository exists with findCurrent, findAsOf, correct, findHistory methods
BitemporalDdlHelper generates correct DDL for columns, constraint, and view
userStories.notes: TDD required. This is infrastructure-level code. For tests, mock JdbcTemplate or use a lightweight in-memory approach. The btree_gist extension must be enabled in PostgreSQL for the GiST exclusion constraint. The 'infinity' value for txn_to is PostgreSQL's special timestamp value. In Java, represent as null or Instant.MAX and convert at the SQL level.

userStories.title: Implement Chain: Planned Order → Actual Order conversion
userStories.description:
Create a handler that converts MRP planned orders into actual purchase orders or production work orders.

Create:
1. plugins/pcba-erp/backend/src/main/java/com/auraboot/plugins/pcbaerp/handler/ConvertPlannedOrderHandler.java

Handles pe:convert_planned_order command.

Logic:
1. Get planned order: db.getById("pe_planned_order", recordId)
2. Check order type: pe_plo_order_type
3. If PURCHASE:
   a. Create pe_purchase_order header:
      - pe_po_code: auto-generate "PO-MRP-{date}-{seq}"
      - pe_po_supplier_id: null (planner assigns later) or from preferred supplier if available
      - pe_po_date: pe_plo_order_date
      - pe_po_status: DRAFT
      - pe_po_remark: "Auto-generated from MRP planned order " + planned order ID
   b. Create pe_purchase_order_line:
      - pe_po_line_product_id: pe_plo_material_id
      - pe_po_line_qty: pe_plo_order_qty
      - pe_po_line_price: 0 (planner fills later)
   c. Update planned order: pe_plo_converted_type = "pe_purchase_order", pe_plo_converted_id = new PO ID

4. If PRODUCTION:
   a. Create pe_production_plan header:
      - pe_pp_code: auto-generate "WO-MRP-{date}-{seq}"
      - pe_pp_product_id: pe_plo_material_id
      - pe_pp_qty: pe_plo_order_qty
      - pe_pp_planned_start: pe_plo_order_date
      - pe_pp_planned_end: pe_plo_need_date
      - pe_pp_status: DRAFT
      - pe_pp_remark: "Auto-generated from MRP planned order"
   b. Update planned order: pe_plo_converted_type = "pe_production_plan", pe_plo_converted_id = new WO ID

5. Return: converted order type + ID

Also update commands.json if pe:convert_planned_order doesn't have handler binding yet.

No separate test file needed — covered by integration tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Planned order updated with converted_type and converted_id
Handler generates meaningful codes for created orders
userStories.notes: This is the MRP→ERP bridge. It converts MRP's planned output into actionable documents. The STATE_TRANSITION pe:convert_planned_order should already exist from Run 1 (US-003) with handler binding. Check if pe_production_plan fields are pe_pp_* prefix. For date auto-generation, use LocalDate.now().format(DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyyMMdd")) + UUID substring.

userStories.title: Integration verification: MRP → APS → MES → Quality end-to-end
userStories.description:
Create an integration verification script/test that validates the complete manufacturing flow works end-to-end.

This is NOT an E2E browser test — it's a backend integration verification that exercises the full chain:

1. Create test data setup:
   - Product with BOM (2-level)
   - Routing with 3 operations (SMT, Reflow, AOI)
   - Resources (1 SMT machine, 1 reflow oven, 1 AOI)
   - Resource calendar (working hours)
   - Inventory (partial stock for some materials)

2. MRP Run:
   - Create demand (simulated sales order)
   - Run MRP engine
   - Verify: planned orders generated for materials with insufficient stock
   - Verify: BOM explosion creates child demands
   - Verify: exceptions for past-due items

3. APS Schedule:
   - Take production planned orders
   - Run scheduling with BottleneckFirst strategy
   - Verify: operations scheduled on correct resources
   - Verify: changeover optimized for same products
   - Verify: Gantt data generated

4. MES Execution:
   - Convert planned production order → work order
   - Confirm work order → operations created from routing
   - Start first operation → actual_start recorded
   - Report work → quantities aggregated
   - Complete operations → yield calculated

5. Quality:
   - FQC order created when last op completes
   - Complete FQC → batch status updated

Create: platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/integration/ManufacturingFlowIntegrationTest.java

This test uses mocked ports/adapters to simulate the full flow without needing a running database. The goal is to verify the SERVICE LAYER integration, not the database layer.

At least 5 test methods covering the 5 stages above.
acceptanceCriteria: Test covers MRP run with BOM explosion and netting
Test covers APS scheduling with resource assignment
Test covers MES work order confirmation and operation execution
Test covers quality FQC creation on production completion
userStories.notes: This is the capstone test. Use @SpringBootTest with mocked adapters (not real DB). Create a comprehensive test fixture with realistic PCBA data: BOM with 5+ materials, routing with 3-4 ops, 2-3 resources. The test should read like a story of the manufacturing flow.

userStories.title: Comprehensive integration and edge case tests
userStories.description:
Add comprehensive tests covering cross-module interactions and edge cases.

Test files:

1. platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/meta/event/EventIntegrationTest.java
- testEventFiredOnCommandCompletion: command → event published
- testEventListenerReceivesPayload: listener gets correct recordId and modelCode
- testMultipleEventsInTransaction: 3 commands → 3 events

2. platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/meta/excel/ExcelEdgeCaseTest.java
- testEmptyExcelFile: 0 rows → empty result
- testMismatchedHeaders: column names don't match any field → validation error
- testLargeDataset: 1000 rows → all imported (performance sanity)

3. platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/meta/bitemporal/BitemporalEdgeCaseTest.java
- testConcurrentCorrections: two corrections of same entity → only latest survives
- testQueryBeforeAnyVersion: asOf before first txnFrom → empty result
- testValidTimeRange: validFrom > validTo → validation error

4. plugins/pcba-erp/backend/src/test/java/com/auraboot/plugins/pcbaerp/handler/ConvertPlannedOrderHandlerTest.java
- testConvertPurchase: PURCHASE type → PO created
- testConvertProduction: PRODUCTION type → work order created
- testConvertSetsBackReference: planned order updated with converted ID
- testConvertUnknownType: unknown type → meaningful error

Total: 15+ new test methods across 4 files.
acceptanceCriteria: All tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test passes
All plugin tests pass: cd plugins/pcba-erp/backend && gradle test passes
userStories.notes: These are the final comprehensive tests. Focus on cross-module boundaries and edge cases. For Excel large dataset test, generate 1000 rows programmatically. For bitemporal concurrent corrections, verify that the older correction gets superseded.
